title: External Developer Feedback Intake — Developer Preview
context: GrantLayer is publicly available on GitHub in a developer-preview / controlled-pilot posture following GL-176 through GL-181. This artifact defines the structured plan for collecting early external developer feedback.

target_reviewer_profiles.label: External Backend Developer
target_reviewer_profiles.description: Familiar with Python, REST APIs, SQLite or PostgreSQL. Can clone a repo, start a backend, and run tests in ~10 minutes.

target_reviewer_profiles.label: AI-Agent Workflow Developer
target_reviewer_profiles.description: Builds or evaluates AI-agent pipelines (LangGraph, LangChain, or similar). Interested in audit trails, verification layers, and agentic compliance.
key_questions: Does the GrantLayer integration story make sense?

target_reviewer_profiles.label: Grant / Compliance or Audit-Minded Reviewer
target_reviewer_profiles.description: Works with grant management, institutional compliance, or audit processes. Does not need to be a developer.
key_questions: Do the concepts (evidence bundles, audit trails, policy evaluation) map to real institutional needs?
Are the caveats about current limitations visible?

target_reviewer_profiles.label: Security-Minded Technical Reviewer
target_reviewer_profiles.description: Reviews security posture of developer-preview software. Familiar with OWASP, API security basics, responsible disclosure.
key_questions: Does the repository look clean of secrets or sensitive paths?

reviewer_tasks.goal: Determine if reviewer can understand what GrantLayer is and does within 2 minutes
Confirm the security reporting channel and current-limitations caveats are visible

reviewer_tasks.task: Run the first verifiable output example
reviewer_tasks.goal: Verify the zero-dependency first output path works from a clean clone
Compare /tmp/grantlayer_first_output.json with examples/first_verifiable_output.json and confirm the record structure is understandable
Confirm 'not production SaaS' and 'tenant isolation not implemented' caveats are visible and understandable

reviewer_tasks.task: Report broken links or confusing docs
reviewer_tasks.goal: Note any link that returns 404 or documentation that is unclear or contradictory
Flag any claim that seems to promise production readiness, tenant isolation, or customer onboarding

feedback_questions.question: Can you understand what GrantLayer does in 2 minutes of reading the README?
What is your one-sentence summary of what GrantLayer is?
Which part of the README felt most confusing?
Can you run the first verifiable output in under 10 minutes?
Were any setup steps missing, unclear, or broken?
Is the expected output understandable? Does the record structure make sense?
Are the safety caveats ('not production SaaS', 'tenant isolation not implemented') easy to find?
Is it clear that this is a developer preview and not a production service?
What would block you from trying the SDK or API next?
Was anything missing that you expected to find?
What would make the repository more credible or trustworthy?
Which next example would you most want to see?
Would you recommend this to a colleague? Why or why not?

triage_categories.label: Blocker
triage_categories.description: Something that prevents the reviewer from completing any task

triage_categories.label: Confusing Docs
triage_categories.description: Documentation that is unclear, contradictory, or hard to follow

triage_categories.label: Broken Quickstart
triage_categories.description: The first verifiable output or clone/run path does not work

triage_categories.label: Missing Example
triage_categories.description: A useful example is absent that would aid understanding

triage_categories.label: Product Question
triage_categories.description: A question about what GrantLayer does or how it works

triage_categories.label: Security Concern
triage_categories.description: A report of a potential security issue — should also be filed via SECURITY.md channel

triage_categories.label: Production Readiness Concern
triage_categories.description: A question or concern about production suitability

triage_categories.label: Feature Request
triage_categories.description: A suggestion for new product functionality

triage_categories.label: Non-Scope / Later
triage_categories.description: Valid feedback that is out of scope for the current developer-preview posture

severity_model.description: Public credential leak, secret material in repository, broken public clone URL, harmful overclaim of production readiness, or SECURITY.md reporting channel broken
Quickstart completely broken, security reporting channel unclear, misleading production SaaS claim, or important safety caveat missing
Confusing setup instructions, unclear caveat placement, important broken link, or misleading but not harmful claim
Minor wording issue, formatting inconsistency, non-critical broken link, or cosmetic confusion
Suggestion, future idea, or neutral observation with no urgency

reviewer_safety_instructions: Do not include secrets or credentials in public issues — use synthetic placeholder values only
Do not include exploit details publicly — use GitHub Security Advisories for security-sensitive reports
Do not include customer data — no real grant applications, institutional records, or personally identifiable information in public issues
Do not include private grants or real institutional funding data — use synthetic identifiers only (e.g. demo-subject-001, test-grant-abc)
Use synthetic data only — all repository examples use synthetic demo data and reviewers should do the same
Security-sensitive reports must be filed via GitHub Security Advisories as described in SECURITY.md

non_goals.description: GrantLayer is in developer-preview / controlled-pilot posture — production SaaS readiness is not claimed
Tenant/workspace isolation is not implemented — stated caveat in README.md and SECURITY.md
No production onboarding process exists yet — not a goal for this developer preview phase
Payment, treasury, and wallet flows are not in current MVP scope
Blockchain integration is planned as optional Phase 3 — not in MVP
No support SLA or uptime guarantee is offered for developer preview
OAuth, JWT, and production authentication are not implemented — developer preview uses static admin token
Collection or storage of real grants or customer data is explicitly not claimed or supported in this posture

feedback_recording_model.escalation_rule: Critical or high severity items must be addressed before inviting the next batch of reviewers
feedback_recording_model.security_rule: Security concerns filed publicly must be acknowledged and redirected to SECURITY.md channel immediately

next_recommended_step.title: External Feedback Triage / Public Issue Hygiene
next_recommended_step.description: Review all feedback received during the GL-182 intake window. Triage each item by category and severity. Identify critical or high-severity items. Establish public GitHub issue labels for ongoing feedback hygiene. Decide whether to add issue templates before widening the reviewer invite list.
next_recommended_step.alternative: GL-183 Public Issue Template / Feedback Labels Readiness — create .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/ entries and GitHub issue labels matching the triage categories above

findings.description: No external feedback has been collected yet — this is the intake plan. Actual findings will be recorded in GL-183 after the first reviewer window.
